tax: Placed at family rank, Molten Magmatics is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a molten flowing rock body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a molten body with its own vent source.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a roiling lava lobe with a cracking crust. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a cooled basalt lobe and scorched track — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Molten Magmatics occupies lava benches and thermal vents. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where vent heat melts a basalt mass and recur with the vent reheats the basalt, sustaining themselves on thermal-vent heat.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: flows to engulf and radiates killing heat. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: molten body and heat-radiance. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: engulfing and heat-radiation. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Molten Magmatics matters because kills by radiant heat on the warm benches. It lets survey reports sort molten hazards from cooled-glass ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Molten Magmatics is the family-rank grouping of liquid-rock fire elementals of the molten kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a flowing molten-rock body that radiates heat and engulfs, and divide below into genus groups by lava chemistry and vent heat. The hottest-bodied of all elementals.
